items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` **Service directory:** `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/` **Triggered by:** 868ee669fc19eb9f3e99a9ea349a2854a08870e7 (PR #57982) by `@ArthurMa1978` ## What Changed The 1.4.0 GA release upgraded the REST API version to `2025-01-01` and updated dependent libraries (Azure.Core 1.52.0, Azure.ResourceManager 1.14.0). The release also deprecated the `ManagedInstanceVcoresCapability.SupportedMemoryLimitsMB` property (marked `[Obsolete]` and `[EditorBrowsable(EditorBrowsableState.Never)]`) in favor of `SupportedMemoryLimitsInMB`, which was not documented in the CHANGELOG. The package also carries AOT compatibility (added in beta.2) and marks obsolete all APIs dependent on the retiring 2014-04-01 API version. ## Gaps Found - **CHANGELOG 1.4.0 entry is sparse:** Does not mention the deprecation of `ManagedInstanceVcoresCapability.SupportedMemoryLimitsMB` in favor of `SupportedMemoryLimitsInMB`, or the persistence of AOT compatibility and the 2014-04-01 API retirement warnings through to the GA release - **README Examples section lacks SQL-specific samples:** The section only links to a generic `(aka.ms/redacted) page; two SQL-specific sample files exist at `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/samples/` (`Sample1_ManagingManagedInstance.md`, `Sample2_ManagingManagedDatabases.md`) but are not referenced in README.md - **README Key concepts section is generic:** Only links to a generic Azure SDK concepts page; it should describe SQL-specific resources managed by this library (SQL servers, databases, elastic pools, managed instances, backup policies, audit policies, encryption protectors, etc.)
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/CHANGELOG.md` Edit the `## 1.4.0 (2026-04-09)` section to add missing details: ```markdown ## 1.4.0 (2026-04-09) ### Features Added - Upgraded api-version to `2025-01-01`. - `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` is AOT-compatible (carried forward from 1.4.0-beta.2). ### Deprecations - `ManagedInstanceVcoresCapability.SupportedMemoryLimitsMB` is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. Use `SupportedMemoryLimitsInMB` instead. - All classes and methods dependent on the retiring 2014-04-01 REST API version are marked obsolete. See (learn.microsoft.com/redacted) for details. ### Other Changes - Upgraded dependent Azure.Core to 1.52.0. - Upgraded dependent Azure.ResourceManager to 1.14.0. ``` #### File 2: `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/README.md` **Key concepts section** — replace the current content with SQL-specific information: ```markdown ## Key concepts The `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` library lets you manage the full lifecycle of Azure SQL resources: - **SQL Servers** (`SqlServerResource`): Create, update, delete, and configure Azure SQL logical servers. - **SQL Databases** (`SqlDatabaseResource`): Create and manage databases within a server, including geo-replicas and point-in-time restore. - **Elastic Pools** (`ElasticPoolResource`): Group databases that share a set of resources. - **Managed Instances** (`ManagedInstanceResource`): Create and manage Azure SQL Managed Instance environments. - **Managed Databases** (`ManagedDatabaseResource`): Databases hosted within a Managed Instance. - **Encryption Protectors** (`EncryptionProtectorResource`): Control Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) keys. - **Audit & Threat Protection Policies**: Configure server/database blob auditing, extended auditing, and Advanced Threat Protection. - **Backup Policies**: Manage short-term and long-term backup retention policies. - **Failover Groups** (`InstanceFailoverGroupResource`): Configure geo-failover groups for high availability. Key concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted) ``` **Examples section** — replace the current content to reference the existing SQL-specific sample files: ```markdown ## Examples The following samples demonstrate common scenarios using this library: - [Managing Managed Instances](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/samples/Sample1_ManagingManagedInstance.md) — Create, list, update, and delete Azure SQL Managed Instances. - [Managing Managed Databases](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/samples/Sample2_ManagingManagedDatabases.md) — Create, list, and delete Managed Databases within a Managed Instance. Additional code samples can be found in the [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippet-backed examples in the README are required for this issue. The existing `Sample1_ManagingManagedInstance.cs` and `Sample2_ManagingManagedDatabases.cs` in `tests/Samples/` already have `#region Snippet:` regions used in the `samples/` markdown files. This issue only adds direct links in `README.md` to those already-existing sample markdown files. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/README.md` already contains all six required sections. The changes above improve: - **Key concepts** — from a generic one-liner to SQL-specific resource descriptions - **Examples** — from a single generic link to direct links to the two existing SQL sample files plus the generic link No sections need to be added; only existing content needs to be enriched. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 sqlmanagement` — confirm no README changes are introduced (snippets are already injected into `samples/` files, not into README directly) 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 sqlmanagement` — no public API surface changed; this is a documentation-only update 5. `dotnet format sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [Sql] Docs: Update CHANGELOG, README key concepts, and examples for 1.4.0 release
